Article Title: Morocco script football history, surpass Italy and Argentina with record 39-game unbeaten streak
Article Text:
Morocco football team. (AP Photo)

A run built on discipline, structure and belief



More than numbers: a footballing transformation



All-time longest unbeaten streaks:

1. Morocco — 39 games (2023-Present)

2. Italy — 37 games (2018-2021)

3. Argentina — 36 games (2019-2022)

4. Algeria — 35 games

4. Spain — 35 games

4. Brazil — 35 games

5. Spain — 32 games (2024-Present)

Surpassing giants of the game



Morocco have etched their name into football folklore after extending their unbeaten run to a staggering 39 matches, setting a new all-time benchmark in international football and overtaking legendary streaks held by Italy and Argentina.The Atlas Lions now stand alone at the top of the global game, surpassing Italy’s celebrated 37-match unbeaten run under Roberto Mancini and Argentina’s 36-game streak that preceded their World Cup triumph in Qatar.What began as a remarkable rise has now become a record-breaking era, with Morocco transforming consistency into history on the international stage.At the heart of Morocco’s extraordinary achievement lies a defensive system that has redefined resilience in modern football. From the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar through to the ongoing 2026 campaign across the United States, Canada and Mexico, the Atlas Lions have built their success on tactical discipline, compact organisation and collective sacrifice.Led by key figures such as Achraf Hakimi, Nayef Aguerd and goalkeeper Yassine Bounou, Morocco’s backline has become one of the most reliable units in world football.Under Walid Regragui, Morocco’s identity has become unmistakable: a side capable of suffering without losing structure, and striking with precision on the counterattack.The foundation was laid in Qatar 2022, where Morocco became the first African and Arab nation to reach a World Cup semi-final, defeating powerhouses including Belgium, Spain and Portugal along the way.Rather than fading after that breakthrough, the Atlas Lions have only grown stronger, blending experienced leaders with emerging talent and maintaining tactical continuity under new coach Mohamed Ouahbi.In a recent 1-0 win over Scotland in Boston in FIFA World Cup 2026, Morocco also completed 601 passes from 671 attempts, recording 59% possession and asserting near-total control — further evidence of their growing dominance in both structure and style.To surpass Italy and Argentina underscores the magnitude of Morocco’s achievement. Italy’s 37-game streak was once considered a modern benchmark of defensive perfection, while Argentina’s 36-match run formed the backbone of their World Cup-winning momentum.Now, Morocco sit above both, rewriting what is possible for teams outside traditional football superpowers.Their unbeaten streak has also eclipsed other elite nations, including Algeria, Spain and Brazil, all of whom have recorded long runs but now trail the new leaders.As the 2026 World Cup continues, Morocco now stand not just as record holders, but as genuine contenders for major international silverware.
Article Summary: Morocco have extended their unbeaten run to a staggering 39 matches. The Atlas Lions now stand alone at the top of the global game. Their unbeaten streak has also eclipsed other elite nations, including Algeria, Spain and Brazil, all of whom have recorded long runs but now trail.

Article Title: 'Not sure people know about it': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes cheeky admission after 29-ball 94 in Tri-Nation final
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Screengrabs)

Tilak Varma praises team's response

Poll What stood out most about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s record-breaking performance? His fearless batting style His ability to perform under pressure

NEW DELHI: Fresh off a record-breaking knock in the Tri-Nation Series final, teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made a cheeky admission about his credentials in 50-over cricket, saying many people may not even realise how much experience he has in the format.The 15-year-old starred in India A's title-clinching victory over Sri Lanka A on Sunday, smashing a stunning 94 off just 29 balls. In the process, he registered the fastest List A fifty in history, reaching the milestone in only 11 deliveries. His explosive start helped India A post 377/9 before dismissing Sri Lanka A for 311 to seal a comfortable 66-run victory.Despite heading into the final after a modest run with the bat and an on-field altercation against the same opposition in the previous game, Sooryavanshi said he never felt under pressure.“I hadn't thought of anything. Just wanted to execute what I planned in the first ten and take it forward from there,” Sooryavanshi said after being named player of the match.Questions had been raised over the youngster's form and temperament after he managed only 117 runs in four innings during the tournament. But Sooryavanshi revealed that discussions with the coaching staff helped him rediscover his rhythm.Watch:“No pressure. I wasn't executing what I wanted. But after consulting with the coaches, I got it right.Learnt a lot this series,” he said.Known mainly for his six-hitting exploits in T20 cricket, the left-hander insisted he was equally comfortable in the longer white-ball format.“I've played a lot of 50-over cricket. Not sure people know about it,” he said.“The challenge was to adapt to different conditions, it was nice to take it on.”India A captain Tilak Varma lauded the team's resilience after bouncing back from consecutive defeats earlier in the competition.“Everyone showed real character. Especially after we lost back-to-back games. Then we won with big margins which was tremendous,” Tilak said.“Everyone has played domestic cricket and has experience. Just a matter of planning before the game. We start big, consolidate and accelerate,” he added.Sri Lanka A skipper Sahan Arachchige acknowledged the impact of Sooryavanshi's innings.“Sooryavanshi played a brilliant innings. We managed the innings nicely thereon but let it slide in the last two overs,” Arachchige said.“When we are chasing a big score, top order can fall cheaply. It happens. Young guys have played well, seniors have scored too. It's been a good tournament,” he added.
Article Summary: India A's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made a cheeky admission about his experience in 50-over cricket. The 15-year-old hit 94 off just 29 balls in the Tri-Nation Series final on Sunday. He registered the fastest List A fifty in history, reaching the milestone in only 11 deliveries.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg: "A squirrel dying in your front yard may be more relevant to your interests right now than…" - what it really reveals about life online
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg

Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg

"A squirrel dying in your front yard may be more relevant to your interests right now than people dying in Africa."

Mark Zuckerberg: The man and the moment behind the quote

What is the meaning behind the quote by Mark Zuckerberg

The catch hidden inside it



How to escape your own bubble

Go looking for what does not come to you. The most important news often will not land in your feed on its own, because it is not relevant by the algorithm's measure. Seek it out on purpose.

Follow sources, not just feeds. Choose a few trusted news outlets and visit them directly now and then, rather than relying only on whatever social media happens to serve you that day.

Notice when everything agrees with you. If all you see online seems to confirm what you already believe, that is a sign of a bubble, not proof that you are right. Deliberately seek out thoughtful views you disagree with.

Remember the squirrel. When something grabs you, pause and ask whether it is truly important or just personally relevant. Knowing the difference helps you spend your limited attention more wisely.

Other famous quotes by Mark Zuckerberg

"Move fast and break things. Unless you are breaking stuff, you are not moving fast enough."

"In a world that's changing really quickly, the only strategy that is guaranteed to fail is not taking risks."

"The question isn't, 'What do we want to know about people?' It's, 'What do people want to tell about themselves?'"

"People think innovation is just having a good idea, but a lot of it is just moving quickly and trying a lot of things."

Seeing past what feels relevant



It is one of the most jarring things a technology leader has ever said, and read quickly, it sounds shockingly cold. A squirrel dying in your front yard, Mark Zuckerberg told colleagues, may matter more to you right now than people dying in Africa. Taken at face value, it seems heartless. But Zuckerberg was not making a claim about which lives are worth more. He was explaining how human attention works, and how the websites we use every day decide what to put in front of us. Understood properly, the quote is less a callous remark and more a window into the hidden machinery that shapes what billions of people see online.Mark Zuckerberg is the co-founder and head of Facebook, now part of the company Meta, one of the largest social media businesses in the world. Zuckerberg said this line while explaining to a journalist how Facebook's news feed decides which stories to show each user.The news feed is that endless stream of posts you scroll through. A central question for Facebook was always this: out of everything happening in the world, what should we show this particular person first? Zuckerberg's answer was that the feed should show whatever is most relevant to you. His squirrel line was simply a blunt, memorable way of explaining what that word relevant really means in practice.Here is the key to the whole thing. Zuckerberg was describing relevance, not importance, and the two are very different.Importance is about how much something matters in the grand scheme of things. By that measure, human lives clearly outweigh a squirrel, and it is not close. Relevance is about how much something connects to your own daily life at this moment. A squirrel dying in your yard is something you would notice, mention to a neighbour, perhaps have to deal with yourself. A tragedy on another continent, however much larger and more important, can feel distant and abstract from where you are sitting. Zuckerberg's point was that people naturally pay more attention to what is close and personal, so a feed built to hold your attention leans toward the close and personal. He was not saying the squirrel matters more. He was saying it feels more relevant, and relevance is what keeps you scrolling.This is where the quote becomes genuinely thought provoking, and where many people start to worry. If the things we see are chosen mainly for personal relevance, what happens to the things that are important but do not feel personally relevant? Distant suffering, complicated issues, opinions we disagree with. They can quietly vanish from view.The writer Eli Pariser gave this problem a name, the filter bubble. The idea is that by constantly showing us what we already like and care about, these systems can wrap each of us in a comfortable bubble, softly hiding the wider world and the views that might challenge us. We end up seeing more of what we want to see and less of what we may actually need to see. Almost by accident, the squirrel quote captured the central tension of the modern internet, the gap between what is relevant to us and what is important for us.The good news is that we are not helpless in the face of this. A few simple habits help us see past what merely feels relevant.Zuckerberg has offered plenty of advice on building, risk and innovation over the years. Here are a few more of his lines.Whatever you make of Mark Zuckerberg or the platforms he built, his squirrel remark has aged into one of the most useful warnings about life online. It pulls back the curtain on a simple truth. The things that grab our attention are not always the ones that deserve it.Feeds are built to feel relevant, because relevance is what keeps us scrolling. But a mind shaped only by what feels immediately relevant becomes a narrow one, slowly cut off from the bigger world outside. The wiser path is to keep one eye on the squirrel in your own front yard, and the other firmly on the wider world beyond it.
Article Summary: Mark Zuckerberg: "A squirrel dying in your front yard may be more relevant to your interests right now than people dying in Africa" He was explaining to a journalist how Facebook's news feed decides which stories to show each user. Zuckerberg was describing relevance, not importance, and the two are very different.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: Theatre Review: A haunting tale of power, desire, betrayal and the cost of obedience
Article Text:
A still from the play The House of Bernarda Alba

The House of Bernarda Alba





Nearly 90 years after Federico García Lorca wrote, the play continues to feel unsettlingly relevant. Directed by Shomik Ray and staged by Shapno Ekhon, this all-women production is a powerful reminder that control and surveillance are not always imposed from outside, they often take root within the home itself.The play unfolds after the death of Bernarda Alba’s husband, when she imposes eight years of mourning on her five daughters. What follows is tragedy about authority, desire, betrayal and the cost of obedience. Watching it today, one is reminded of philosopher Paulo Freire’s observation that the oppressed become oppressors and go on to reproduce them. Bernarda is not merely an authoritarian figure; she is also a custodian of a system that hasshaped her.One dialogue, in particular, lingers long after the curtain falls. “The only right you have is the right of obedience,” says Bernarda to her daughters – it captures the essence of the play. It is a statement that transcends the household and speaks to every structure that values conformity over freedom. The strength of the production lies in its portrayal of the sisters. The jealousy and animosity between them are conveyed with conviction.Rather than uniting against Bernarda’s rule, the daughters become trapped in rivalries fuelled by confinement and frustration, reflecting how repressive systems fracture social relations. The set design reinforces a sense of confinement, with windows serving as reminders of a world that can be seen but not accessed.Adding to it is an evocative musical score by Subhashish Sam that heightens the atmosphere of tension and anticipation running through the play. While rooted in rural Spain, its themes of surveillance, reputation, conformity and control remain recognisable across societies. I wished the production had pushed a little further into the play’s broader political resonances. Bernarda’s household often feels like a metaphor for authoritarian systems, and a sharper emphasis on that dimension could have added more punch.Want us to review your play?Mail us at tims.delhitimes@timesofindia.com
Article Summary: Federico García Lorca wrote The House of Bernarda Alba 90 years ago. The all- women production is a powerful reminder that control and surveillance are not always imposed from outside, they often take root within the home. The strength of the production lies in its portrayal of the sisters.

Article Title: 4 youngsters trapped in elevator of Navi Mumbai high-rise for 90 minutes; rescued safely by firemen
Article Text: NAVI MUMBAI: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage in the residential area.The Kharghar fire brigade received the emergency call at 4.25 am and reached the society at 4.35 am.Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside using a crowbar and a battery-operated spreader tool.Prashant Darekar, a leading fireman at Kharghar, said,"It took us less than 5 minutes to rescue the trapped youngsters. While, one woman was a tenant in the building, the other three were her friends who had come to drop her home after they returned from a night out."
Article Summary: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage. Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside.

Article Title: IIT B grad skipped placements to join FTII, wins Silver Conch at MIFF
Article Text:
Shubham Sumit wins the Silver Conch for Best Indian Short Fiction Film for his FTII diploma film Small Clouds at the 19th MIFF

Pune:





Shubham Sumit had learned to imagine films much before he had ever watched one.Two decades ago, in Buxar (Bihar), Sumit used to lie on the terrace of his house on summer nights. Since electricity arrived in the town only in the last decade, he would hear movies playing on TV being run by tractor batteries on his neighbour’s terrace. The dialogues and songs drifting through the darkness would fill him with wonder. On Sunday, Sumit (29) won the Silver Conch for Best Indian Short Fiction Film for his FTII diploma film Small Clouds at the 19th Mumbai International Film Festival (MIFF). Sumit has journeyed far from hearing movies in the dark to being celebrated for making one. Now his ambition is to make feature films.Sumit took a small detour to earn a chemical engineering degree from IIT Bombay before becoming a filmmaker. Instead of appearing for placements, he joined the masters course in direction and screenplay at FTII, passing in 2025. He works for Guneet Monga ’s production house Sikhya Entertainment, currently.This year, FTII won big at MIFF — with its students and alumni getting various awards. Milan Kumar won the IDPA award for Best Student Film (national competition) for The Old Bull Knows or Once Knew.Randhir (Ranadhir) Biswas won the Best Cinematography Award (national competition) for Small Clouds. Alumnus Sainath S Uskaikar won the Silver Conch for Best Indian Documentary Film Waai. MIFF is the oldest and largest film festival for non-feature films in South Asia.Growing up in a lower middle class farming household, Sumit loved stories. “My twin sister and I would buy pulp novels for Rs5 from a man who would roam around with a bag of books. It was our only access to stories, other than textbooks. The village was so behind that electricity arrived only in 2014. Also, watching movies was considered bad,” said Sumit. He remembered his father renting a TV and CD player because Sumit had to appear for a quiz competition in Patna. One round was to identify actors from a scene or the movie. “We just fast forward everything to recognise the actors and movies for four days,” he said.In Class VI, he moved to Jharkhand’s Netarhat Residential School, which opened a world of literature and politics to him. He topped Class X. Later, Sumit — who wanted to become a novelist — was told by everyone that IIT was the best way ahead and he joined the Chemistry dept at IIT Bombay.There he found a tribe that loved poetry, theatre and films. “I watched Pather Panchali for the first time and found my calling,” said Sumit, who says his decision to not take a cushy job and join FTII in 2020 shocked his parents. “They have no idea about films and it took them time to understand my decision.”Covid pandemic meant he could not attend college for almost two years, finally completing his course in 2025. “FTII was a revelation. It taught me to not just accept, but question everything. I learnt nothing is simple, you have to peel the layers and look at things from all angles,” he said.Meanwhile, his diploma film Small Clouds is a group effort between him and four of his batchmates. “The story is about fraternal twins — a brother and sister — on the verge of their teens. They escape into a world of adventure, but conflicts arise when they start to grow up. National Film Development Corporation of India called it a ‘moving exploration of childhood, the film touched hearts through its emotional depth and evocative storytelling’ in its X post.The story comes from a place of memory, experiences and nostalgia. “I am still scared to show it to my twin sister. I don’t know how she will react,” said Sumit.Among other student films, Madhurima Maiti received a grant of Rs1 lakh for her documentary project The Girl Beneath The Sun at the 2nd WAVES Doc Bazaar held alongside MIFF 2026.The alumni who made their presence felt were Bigyna Dahal for Best Sound Design Award under the National Competition category for film Kovarty. Pooja Tolani received the Dadasaheb Phalke Chitranagari Award for Best Debut Director for film Raaza. Additionally, the Best Short Film Award on Viksit Bharat/ 150 Years of Vande Mataram/ Bharat@2047 was awarded to The Last Shelter and received by alumnus Rajesh Bhatia and Bharat Arora.
Article Summary: Shubham Sumit won the Silver Conch for Best Indian Short Fiction Film for his FTII diploma film Small Clouds at the 19th Mumbai International Film Festival (MIFF) Now his ambition is to make feature films. This year, FTII won big at MIFF — with its students and alumni getting various awards.

Article Title: Young chess talent finishes second in Maharashtra U-13 Championship
Article Text:
U-13 Maharashtra chess runner-up

Pune:





A Class VII student has brought laurels to his school by securing second place in the Under-13 Maharashtra State Chess Championship.Lathik Raam Sundar delivered an impressive performance against some of the state’s most talented young chess players, displaying sharp strategic skills and remarkable composure throughout the competition. His success has earned him the opportunity to represent Maharashtra at the National Chess Championship, a significant milestone in his sporting journey.School authorities praised Lathik’s dedication, discipline and passion for chess, describing the achievement as a result of sustained hard work and perseverance. His parents thanked the school and teachers for their constant encouragement and support.The achievement has been hailed as an inspiration for fellow students, demonstrating that determination, focused practice and commitment can help young talents excel at the highest levels of competition.
Article Summary: Lathik Raam Sundar secured second place in the Under-13 Maharashtra State Chess Championship. His success has earned him the opportunity to represent Maharashtra at the National Chess Championship, a significant milestone in his sporting journey. School authorities praised Lathik's dedication, discipline and passion for chess.

Article Title: Double delight for Utsav & Varda, Jayesh regains men’s title
Article Text:
Podium finishers of the Shivaji Nagar open ranking table tennis tournament with the guests

Nagpur:





Utsav Meshram and Varda Kejgir clinched twin titles while Jayesh Kulkarni outclassed Vaibhav Rane 11-7, 11-6, 11-5 to regain the men’s crown in the Shivaji Nagar open ranking table tennis tournament on Sunday.After registering a facile 3-1 triumph over Karan Kashyap in the U-15 boys singles final, Utsav rallied from two games down to get the better of Manas Sharma in the U-17 summit clash to secure twin titles. Utsav won the U-17 final 7-11, 6-11, 11-6, 11-7, 11-1.Similarly, Varda posted a facile 3-1 triumph over Purvi Kaur Renu in the U-19 final before recording a come-from-behind victory over Bhumika Malode in the women’s title clash. Varda lifted the women’s crown after losing the first game 7-11, 11-4, 11-4, 11-6.Later Shekhar Tongo distributed the prizes. On the occasion, Ashutosh Potnis, Ajay Kamble, Rajesh Mopkar and Deepak Kanetkar were present.RESULTS (All Finals):U-11 Girls: Saiee Gunjal bt Sharyu Khobragade 11-6, 11-8;U-11 Boys: Sidhan Gour bt Reyansh Pendharkar 10-12, 13-11, 11-8;U-13 Girls: Sirin Meshram bt Prashabdi Shymkuwar 6-11, 11-4, 11-7;U-13 Boys: Laksh Chandak bt Adwait Daga 11-3, 11-7U-15 Girls: Kevika Nandeshwar bt Purvi Kaur Renu 11-9, 12-10, 11-5;U-15 Boys: Utsav Meshram bt Karan Kashyap 11-9, 10-12, 11-9, 15-13;U-17 Girls: Purvi Kaur Renu bt Sirin Meshram 7-11, 11-7, 11-5, 7-11, 11-9;U-17 Boys: Utsav Meshram bt Manas Sharma 7-11, 6-11, 11-6, 11-7, 11-1;U-19 Girls: Varda Kejgir bt Purvi Kaur Renu 11-7, 11-8, 10-12, 11-9;U-19 Boys: Sarth Shastri bt Utsav Meshram 11-2, 11-9, 11-7;Women: Varda Kejgir bt Bhumika Malode 7-11, 11-4, 11-4, 11-6;Men: Jayesh Kulkarni bt Vaibhav Rane 11-7, 11-6, 11-5.
Article Summary: Utsav Meshram and Varda Kejgir clinched twin titles. Jayesh Kulkarni outclassed Vaibhav Rane to regain the men’s crown in the Shivaji Nagar open ranking table tennis tournament on Sunday.

Article Title: Cross-voting rocks Nashik seat: BJP rebel Gokul Gite stuns Mahayuti in MLC polls upset
Article Text:
Gokul Gite and his supporters celebrate his poll victory against Mahayuti nominee Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities constituency





NASHIK: BJP rebel and independent candidate Gokul Gite’s win over the Mahayuti nominee Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities constituency is just a pointer to how cross-voting played out in the 11 seats that went to the polls on June 18.The results, announced on Saturday, had Mahayuti emerging victorious in 10 of the seats, but there was a strong undercurrent of croass-voting. In fact, cross-voting in places like Satara, Sangli, Nagpur and Akola showed the alliance couldn’t stop its members in spite of a majority. Marathwada saw a contrast with cross-voting boosting BJP-Sena margins in three of four seats. Mahayuti had already won six of the 17 MLC seats unopposed.In Nashik, Gite defeated the Mahayuti candidate, despite the Darade camp claiming a commanding share of 473 out of 618 votes. Gite secured 357 votes, surpassing Darade by 109 and comfortably crossing the preferential victory quota of 303 to seal an unexpected win when votes were counted at the Nashik district collectorate on Monday. The result flew in the face of weeks of political manoeuvring and tight control measures by Mahayuti, including “resort politics”, to prevent cross-voting.Yet, the final tally breached every safeguard.The contest had drawn attention from the outset after brothers, Ganesh and Gokul Gite, entered the fray as independents against Darade. While Ganesh Gite, a former NMC standing committee chairman and close confidante of WRD minister Girish Mahajan, withdrew, Gokul Gite remained in the race despite sustained pressure. Three senior ministers — Girish Mahajan, Uday Samant and Dada Bhuse — camped in Nashik for nearly six days to secure his withdrawal, eventually extracting only a partial climbdown.“I never said I supported an inappropriate and incompatible candidate like Narendra Darade, who failed to fulfil the aspirations of his voters throughout his tenure as MLC,” Gokul Gite said after the win. “I only stopped my campaign, but activists and voters from across the district supported me anyway.”As counting trends turned decisive, Darade and his family members, including son Kunal and brother Kishor, left the venue abruptly. A visibly upset Darade alleged betrayal from within his own alliance. “All the functionaries and workers of Mahayuti, except deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde , have betrayed me. They kept me in the dark while supporting my opponent,” he said.Kishor Darade echoed the charge, pointing to internal sabotage. “We had called for a straight fight. Netas from Mumbai too intervened, saying a contest would set wrong precedents. Now we understand what that was actually about,” he said.Gokul Gite, meanwhile, claimed even family ties were leveraged against him to sway voters, but all failed. “Deepali Gite (Ganesh Gite’s wife), an elected member, had appealed to voters to support Darade during the campaign. But the voters were determined to cast their ballots in my favour,” Gokul added.Water resources minister Girish Mahajan admitted surprise at the result, but denied allegations of betrayal. “We have not betrayed anybody. I know Darade is feeling distressed right now. We were relying on Gokul’s exit, which he apparently did not fully execute. We will carry out an internal introspection,” he said. Minister Uday Samant said a detailed report would be submitted to deputy CM Eknath Shinde.Historically, Nashik’s MLC seat has often thrown up winners from outside the ruling establishment, and Gite’s victory continues that trend, joining past upsets involving the likes of Vasant Pawar and Devidas Pingle..Absence of whip countedThe upset has once again underscored the unpredictability of local authorities’ elections conducted through preferential voting and secret ballot. Without the enforceability of a party whip, cross-voting becomes difficult to detect or prevent—a factor Mahayuti functionaries had feared from the start. That is way they had worked so hard to get Gite out of the race.“In the absence of a whip, a nominated party official who ensures voting discipline, there is always a high chance of cross-voting, which is exactly what happened here,” a senior BJP office-bearer said on condition of anonymity. “BJP’s Narendra Mahajan logs big win in JalgaonIn sharp contrast to Nashik, BJP’s Narendra Mahajan registered a landslide victory in Jalgaon, polling 582 of 611 valid votes. Shiv Sena (UBT) candidate Sharad Tayade got 17 votes, independent Anil Chaudhary secured 11, while rebel Reshma Kale managed just one vote.Kale, who stopped campaigning to support Mahajan, the Mahayuti candidate, expressed regret after hearing the Nashik results. “We put our faith in BJP’s commitment to support the Mahayuti candidate in Nashik, which is why I had stepped back in Jalgaon. After seeing the Nashik result, I wish I had contested with full commitment.”
Article Summary: Gokul Gite defeated Mahayuti candidate Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities constituency. Gite secured 357 votes, surpassing Darade by 109 and comfortably crossing the preferential victory quota of 303. The result flew in the face of weeks of political manoeuvring and tight control measures by Mahayutis.
